questions.prompt: A customer reports 'my call this morning dropped after a few minutes'. You filter CDR (Advanced) to the right time window and find the call. Status: ANSWERED. Duration: 6:42. What's the next step?
choices: Click into the call detail and open Timeline View to see the chronological events that ended the call
Send the user a fresh welcome email
questions.explanation: Timeline View gives chronological events: hold start/end, transfers, who hung up, what triggered the BYE. Often shows the call ended normally (caller hung up) and there's no real problem to fix. SIP trace is the next step *if* Timeline View doesn't explain it.

questions.prompt: Which CDR Status value would you see for a call where the caller hung up before anyone answered?
questions.explanation: ABANDONED is the CDR status for 'caller hung up before answer'. ANSWERED = someone picked up; BUSY = called party was busy; VOICEMAIL = call routed to voicemail; FAILED = something else went wrong.
